subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Types of Open Wounds
description: A 60-second lesson on Types of Open Wounds.

hook.text: A laceration, an incision, and an avulsion look different because the mechanism of injury differs.

buildup.text: Abrasions scrape the surface. Lacerations tear with jagged edges. Incisions cut clean lines.

discovery.text: Punctures are deep and narrow—they seal on the surface but may be infected deep inside.

twist.text: Avulsions rip tissue away completely. Degloving injuries peel skin off the underlying structure.

climax.text: Each wound type has different bleeding, infection, and healing characteristics requiring different care.

punchline.text: The shape of the wound tells you what made it and how it will heal.

quiz.question: Why are puncture wounds particularly dangerous despite looking small?
options: They seal on the surface but can be infected deep inside
